items.headline: NBA Game 1 Highlights: Lakers 107, Rockets 98
items.blurb: NBA Game 1 Highlights: Lakers 107, Rockets 98 — Luke Kennard poured in a game-high 27 points on nearly 70 per cent shooting, while LeBron James posted 19 points and 13 assists as the Los Angeles Lakers outlasted the Houston Rockets 107-98.

items.headline: Unlikely hero Luke Kennard leads Lakers to Game 1 win over Rockets
items.blurb: Unlikely hero Luke Kennard leads Lakers to Game 1 win over Rockets — With Luka Doncic and Austin Reaves both out of action and unlikely to return in the series, the Los Angeles Lakers were led by an unlikely hero on Saturday night.

items.headline: Lakers’ LeBron James, Kennard drill back-to-back threes to extend lead
items.blurb: Lakers’ LeBron James, Kennard drill back-to-back threes to extend lead — Watch as Los Angeles Lakers forwards LeBron James and Luke Kennard drill back-to-back threes against the Houston Rockets to extend their fourth-quarter lead in Game 1.

items.headline: Stephen Curry puts pressure on Warriors front office with cryptic comments
items.blurb: Stephen Curry puts pressure on Warriors front office with cryptic comments — The Golden State Warriors’ late-season playoff push ended abruptly with a 111-96 road loss to the Phoenix Suns.

items.headline: James Harden passes Larry Bird on playoff scoring list in Cavaliers' blowout Game 1 win
items.blurb: James Harden passes Larry Bird on playoff scoring list in Cavaliers' blowout Game 1 win — The victory marked a successful franchise playoff debut for guard James Harden, who moved up the all-time playoff scoring list.

items.headline: NBA Game 1 Highlights: Knicks 113, Hawks 102
items.blurb: NBA Game 1 Highlights: Knicks 113, Hawks 102 — Jalen Brunson scored 28 points and dished out seven assists, Karl-Anthony Towns added 25 points of his own as the New York Knicks defeated the Atlanta Hawks 113-102 in Game 1 of their first-round series.

items.headline: Nikola Jokic, Jamal Murray show why Nuggets are still NBA title contenders
items.blurb: Nikola Jokic, Jamal Murray show why Nuggets are still NBA title contenders — Do not forget about the Denver Nuggets, because they are still here, they are still relevant, and they still have a championship duo that is perfectly capable of putting the team on their backs and carrying it to greatness.

items.headline: Braylon Mullins Returning To UConn For Sophomore Season
items.blurb: Braylon Mullins Returning To UConn For Sophomore Season — Braylon Mullins is returning to the University of Connecticut for his sophomore season. Mullins was a player to watch as a potential Early Entry Candidate for the 2026 NBA Draft. ESPN ranked Mullins 17th on their latest Top 100 prospects list. Most expected the 6-foot-6 guard to be drafted somewhere from the late-lottery to mid-20s, had he

items.headline: Kevin Durant To Miss Game 1 Vs Lakers With Right Knee Injury
items.blurb: Kevin Durant To Miss Game 1 Vs Lakers With Right Knee Injury — Houston Rockets forward Kevin Durant will miss Game 1 vs the Los Angeles Lakers due to a right knee injury Rockets coach Ime Udoka said that Durant banged knees with a teammate during practice this week. Udoka added that Durant being out is "Hopefully it's a one-game thing". The Lakers are down both Luka Doncic and Austin Reaves. That

items.headline: Rockets’ Kevin Durant misses Game 1 vs. Lakers
items.blurb: Rockets’ Kevin Durant misses Game 1 vs. Lakers

items.headline: All-Star duo leads Nuggets to ugly Game 1 win over Timberwolves
items.blurb: All-Star duo leads Nuggets to ugly Game 1 win over Timberwolves — The Denver Nuggets couldn't make threes, lost Aaron Gordon to foul trouble and fell behind by double digits early. Thanks to patience, defense and their All-Star duo, Denver took Game One over the Minnesota Timberwolves anyway.

items.headline: Murray, Jokic lead Nuggets to win over Timberwolves in physical opener
items.blurb: Murray, Jokic lead Nuggets to win over Timberwolves in physical opener — Jamal Murray scored 30 points, going 16 of 16 from the free-throw line, and Nikola Jokic had a triple-double as the Denver Nuggets shook off a sluggish start to beat the Minnesota Timberwolves 116-105 on Saturday in the opener of their first-round playoff series.

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Nuggets 116, Timberwolves 105
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Nuggets 116, Timberwolves 105 — Canada’s Jamal Murray posted a game-high 30 points, and Nikola Jokic recorded a triple-double with 25 points, 13 rebounds, and 11 assists as the Denver Nuggets defeated the Minnesota Timberwolves 116-105.

items.headline: How Cavaliers’ experience and talent overwhelmed Raptors in Game 1
items.blurb: How Cavaliers’ experience and talent overwhelmed Raptors in Game 1 — Danielle Michaud and Michael Grange discuss the Toronto Raptors blowout loss to the Cleveland Cavaliers in Game 1, explaining the Cavs experience was on full display.

items.headline: ‘Me shooting nine shots is not going to win’: Ingram on second-half usage
items.blurb: ‘Me shooting nine shots is not going to win’: Ingram on second-half usage — Toronto Raptors’ Brandon Ingram explains how he was used as a screener in the loss to the Cleveland Cavaliers, saying they’re “not going to win basketball games” if he only takes nine shots.

items.headline: Timberwolves’ Edwards puts up nasty switch-hand layup
items.blurb: Timberwolves’ Edwards puts up nasty switch-hand layup — Watch as Minnesota Timberwolves star Anthony Edwards goes up for a nasty switch-hand layup against the Denver Nuggets.

items.headline: Jalen Green Scores 36 As Suns Eliminate Warriors, Claim No. 8 Seed
items.blurb: Jalen Green Scores 36 As Suns Eliminate Warriors, Claim No. 8 Seed — Jalen Green delivered 36 points on 14-of-20 shooting as the Phoenix Suns eliminated the Golden State Warriors 111-96 Friday, claiming the Western Conference's No. 8 seed and a first-round playoff matchup against defending champion Oklahoma City Thunder beginning Sunday. Green, who averaged just 13.3 points on 37% shooting against

items.headline: Immanuel Quickley Misses Game 1 With Strained Hamstring
items.blurb: Immanuel Quickley Misses Game 1 With Strained Hamstring — Immanuel Quickley was held out of the Toronto Raptors' 126-113 loss in Game 1 of their first round series against the Cleveland Cavaliers due to a right hamstring injury. Quickley suffered the injury during last Sunday's regular season finale. Darko Rajakovic said Quickley is making progress and could return for Game 2 on Monday. The Raptor

items.headline: Adam Silver may have revealed LeBron James' plans for next season
items.blurb: Adam Silver may have revealed LeBron James' plans for next season — LeBron James will close his 23rd NBA season with an opportunity to keep stacking the bricks of an almost flawless résumé.

items.headline: Giannis Antetokounmpo is already flirting with one team, shutting down another
items.blurb: Giannis Antetokounmpo is already flirting with one team, shutting down another — Once again, Giannis Antetokounmpo will be one of the most talked-about players in the NBA this offseason.

items.headline: Bucks To Cast Wide Net In Search For Next Coach
items.blurb: Bucks To Cast Wide Net In Search For Next Coach — The Milwaukee Bucks are looking for a new head coach after parting ways with Doc Rivers after the season. For the first time since 2018, GM Jon Horst is leading a real search, since the last two hires were guarantees for the Bucks. Adrian Griffin was widely reported to be the preferred candidate of Giannis Antetokounmpo, and Rivers was the only one

items.headline: What went wrong for Raptors in Game 1 vs. Cavaliers
items.blurb: What went wrong for Raptors in Game 1 vs. Cavaliers — Brad Fay, Matt Bonner, and Sherman Hamilton break down what went wrong for the Toronto Raptors in their Game 1 blowout loss to the Cleveland Cavaliers.

items.headline: Trail Blazers Not Looking To Spend Big Money On A Head Coach
items.blurb: Trail Blazers Not Looking To Spend Big Money On A Head Coach — The Portland Trail Blazers may move on from Tiago Splitter, despite the interim head coach leading the team to their first playoff appearance in five years. If they Blazers do go for a new coach, they aren't looking to spend big money to bring one to Portland. New Trail Blazers owner Tom Dundon apparently doesn't want to spend more tha

items.headline: NBA Game 1 Highlights: Cavaliers 126, Raptors 113
items.blurb: NBA Game 1 Highlights: Cavaliers 126, Raptors 113 — Donovan Mitchell continued his success in NBA Playoff Game 1’s by dropping 32 points on 11-for-20 shooting to lead the Cleveland Cavaliers past the Toronto Raptors 126-113 in Game 1.

items.headline: Cavaliers impose their will to dominate Raptors in Game 1
items.blurb: Cavaliers impose their will to dominate Raptors in Game 1 — You could argue the Raptors were ready for Game 1, and it didn’t matter. The Raptors have struggled all season against the NBA’s best teams, and their early-season success against Cleveland aside, the Cavaliers very much look like one of those teams.

items.headline: Raptors’ Barnes drills back-to-back threes to start Game 1 vs. Cavs
items.blurb: Raptors’ Barnes drills back-to-back threes to start Game 1 vs. Cavs — Watch as Toronto Raptors superstar Scottie Barnes starts Game 1 hot from beyond the arc, drilling two threes against the Cleveland Cavaliers.

items.blurb: Why “Playoff BI” is ready to lead Raptors past Cavaliers — Toronto Raptors’ Brandon Ingram sits down with Danielle to break down the challenge they face going against the Cleveland Cavaliers and his mindset heading into the NBA Playoffs.

items.headline: Draymond Green gets ejected in what could be final game with Warriors
items.blurb: Draymond Green gets ejected in what could be final game with Warriors — If Friday was Draymond Green's final game with the Golden State Warriors, he went out in classic Draymond fashion.

items.headline: Draymond Green, Devin Booker ejected in final minute of Suns' blowout win
items.blurb: Draymond Green, Devin Booker ejected in final minute of Suns' blowout win — Draymond Green made sure to go out with a bang on Friday as the Golden State Warriors’ season came to an end at the hands of Devin Booker and the Phoenix Suns.

items.headline: NBA Western Conference playoffs burning questions: How do juggernauts fare?
items.blurb: NBA Western Conference playoffs burning questions: How do juggernauts fare? — As the NBA playoffs loom, the Western Conference has fractured into a clear hierarchy where the defending champion Thunder, a surging Spurs squad led by Victor Wembanyama, and a historically efficient Nuggets offence stand as the titans to beat.

items.headline: Steve Kerr Unsure If He'll Return As Warriors Head Coach Next Season
items.blurb: Steve Kerr Unsure If He'll Return As Warriors Head Coach Next Season — Steve Kerr acknowledged the possibility of not returning as head coach of the Golden State Warriors on Friday following their loss at the Phoenix Suns in the play-in. Kerr is without a contract for the 2026-27 season, which was a purposeful decision as he evaluates his future. "I don't know what's going to happen," Kerr said. "

items.headline: Stephen Curry Interested In Signing Extension With Warriors
items.blurb: Stephen Curry Interested In Signing Extension With Warriors — Stephen Curry has one season remaining on his contract at $62.6 million for 2026-27 with the Golden State Warriors and he expressed interest in signing an extension. Curry, who most recently signed the one-year extension in 2024, has yet to hold talks with the Warriors. Curry was also asked if he can envision himself playing more than j

items.headline: NBA Eastern Conference Playoffs burning questions: Can lower seeds stage upsets?
items.blurb: NBA Eastern Conference Playoffs burning questions: Can lower seeds stage upsets? — Odds of an early upset in the East may seem low after the top teams idly watched as those below played musical chairs. But Zulfi Sheikh asks one burning question for each first-round series to figure out if any intrigue may be in store.

items.headline: Jalen Brunson: Leads Knicks in Game 1
items.blurb: Jalen Brunson: Leads Knicks in Game 1 — Brunson logged 28 points (9-22 FG, 3-4 3Pt, 7-8 FT), seven assists, five rebounds and one steal in 36 minutes of Saturday's 113-102 Game 1 win over the Hawks. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Kevin Durant: Won't play in Game 1
items.blurb: Kevin Durant: Won't play in Game 1 — Durant (knee) has been ruled out for Saturday's Game 1 against the Lakers, Brett Siegel of ClutchPoints.com reports. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Anthony Edwards: Nets 22 points with full line
items.blurb: Anthony Edwards: Nets 22 points with full line — Edwards logged 22 points (7-19 FG, 2-9 3Pt, 6-7 FT), nine rebounds, seven assists and three blocks over 38 minutes during Saturday's 116-105 loss to the Nuggets in Game 1 of the first round of the Western Conference playoffs. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Nikola Jokic: Triple-doubles in Game 1 win
items.blurb: Nikola Jokic: Triple-doubles in Game 1 win — Jokic supplied 25 points (11-19 FG, 2-7 3Pt, 1-1 FT), 13 rebounds, 11 assists and one steal across 40 minutes during Saturday's 116-105 win over Minnesota in Game 1 of the first round of the Western Conference playoffs. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Jamal Murray: Nets game-high 30 points in win
items.blurb: Jamal Murray: Nets game-high 30 points in win — Murray recorded 30 points (7-22 FG, 0-8 3Pt, 16-16 FT), five rebounds and seven assists across 39 minutes during Saturday's 116-105 victory over the Timberwolves in Game 1 of the first round of the playoffs. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.
